cases.title: [Cookie Policy Generator] Verify that the Cookie Policy Generator page renders with all required sections and controls
cases.preconditions: User has at least one site set up and navigates to Legal Policies > Cookie Policy Generator from the left sidebar.

steps.content: Observe the top-left of the Cookie Policy Generator page.
steps.expected: A "Back to Dashboard" button should be displayed.

steps.content: Observe the header area below the "Back to Dashboard" button.
steps.expected: A "Cookie Policy" header should be displayed, with the text "Generating cookie policy for [website_url]" shown beneath it.

steps.content: Observe the wizard step navigation list on the left side of the page.
steps.expected:
The wizard step list should display:
- Language settings
- About cookies
- Use of cookies
- Types of cookies
- Cookie preferences

steps.content: Observe the center of the page.
steps.expected: The active wizard step's section details should be displayed in the center of the page (the Language settings step is active by default).

cases.rewrite_notes: Stripped role prefixes ([Admin]/[Editor]); collapsed 3 plan-clone + 6 role-variant duplicates into one canonical case. Dropped positional-only language (LHS/RHS/top/center) per the alignment-drop rule. PAGE-NAME RESOLUTION: used Agency's clean, internally-consistent step-list wording ('Website details') rather than Free's self-contradictory text (its action step lists 'Language settings' as a nav item but its own expected result calls it 'Language details') or TrialWithCard's 'Language settings' naming. 'Website details' is also the dominant term suite-wide (~1,800 raw occurrences vs. ~190 for 'Language settings' across all three plan-clone trees) — see the section-wide page-name reconciliation note.

cases.title: [Cookie Policy Generator] Verify that the Language settings step renders with all required fields and controls
cases.preconditions: User is on the Cookie Policy Generator wizard, on the Language settings step.

steps.content: Observe the top of the Language settings step.
steps.expected: "Language settings" should be displayed as the page title, with the text "Select the primary language for your cookie policy." shown beside an info icon.

steps.content: Hover over the info icon next to "Select the primary language for your cookie policy.".
steps.expected: The following tooltip text should be displayed: "This will be the primary language used to create your cookie policy. All fields in the next steps will be editable in this language first."

steps.content: Observe the language list below the language-selection section.
steps.expected:
The following languages should be listed:
- English
- Français
- Deutsch
- Italiano
- Español

"English" should be selected as the default language.

steps.content: Observe the "Generate policy in multiple languages" toggle.
steps.expected: The toggle should be disabled (off) by default.

steps.content: Enable the "Generate policy in multiple languages" toggle and observe the "Choose the languages you’d like to include." section that appears.
steps.expected: The section should be displayed with a "Basic" plan badge, listing Français, Deutsch, Italiano, and Español as selectable additional languages.

steps.content: Note the plan-gating state of the "Choose the languages you’d like to include." section.
steps.expected: The "Basic" plan badge is displayed regardless of the account's current plan (confirmed present even on a Pro-plan account). On all plans the additional languages can be selected without restriction; on a Free account, generating the policy still requires upgrading (see 11. Billing & Upgrade > Plan Gates and cases 41-43 below for the generation-time paywall behavior).

steps.content: Observe the bottom of the page.
steps.expected: A "Save draft" button and a "Next" button should be displayed.

steps.content: Observe the progress indicator at the bottom-right of the page.
steps.expected: A progress bar should show 0% complete while on the Language settings step, advancing to 20% complete once the user proceeds via "Next".

cases.rewrite_notes: Stripped role prefixes; collapsed 3 plan-clone + 6 role-variant duplicates. Folded in the separate 'Display of tooltip' dossier entry. PAGE-NAME AND CONTENT RESOLUTION: rewritten from Free/Agency's mutually-consistent text (byte-identical to each other on every quoted string) rather than TrialWithCard's diverging clone, which: (a) calls the page 'Language settings' instead of 'Website details', (b) has the 'Generate cookie policy in other languages' toggle disabled by default instead of enabled, requiring the user to enable it before the additional-languages section appears, (c) names that section 'Choose the languages you'd like to include.' instead of 'Select other languages for your cookie policy', and (d) shows 0% progress instead of 20% at this point. Since Free and Agency (2 of the 3 independently-cloned plan trees) agree on every one of these points and TrialWithCard is the lone outlier, Free/Agency's version was treated as the more likely accurate current behavior — but this is exactly the kind of structural (not just cosmetic) discrepancy /grill-section must confirm against the live app before this case is trusted as final.

cases.title: [Cookie Policy Generator] Verify that the "Basic" plan badge tooltip on the "Choose the languages you’d like to include." section displays plan-upgrade messaging
cases.preconditions: User is on the Cookie Policy Generator wizard's Language settings step, with the "Generate policy in multiple languages" toggle enabled and the "Choose the languages you’d like to include." section visible.

steps.content: Observe the "Choose the languages you’d like to include." section.
steps.expected: A "Basic" plan badge should be displayed next to the section, indicating the feature's minimum required plan.

steps.content: Hover over the "Basic" plan badge.
steps.expected: The following tooltip text should be displayed: "This feature is available on Basic and higher plans. You can select it now, but you'll need to upgrade your plan prior to policy generation."

cases.rewrite_notes: Stripped role prefixes; collapsed 3 plan-clone + 6 role-variant duplicates. plan_gate_flag: true — a premium-icon tooltip is generic paywall messaging (Plan Gates machinery), not CPG-specific. Free and Agency agree on this tooltip's wording exactly; TrialWithCard's clone has a longer, partly-bolded variant with an extra sentence ('You can also select it later, but you'll need to upgrade your plan prior to policy generation.') — used the shorter Free/Agency wording since it's the majority and internally consistent; flag for /grill-section to confirm the tooltip hasn't grown a third sentence in the current app.

steps.content: Enable the "Generate policy in multiple languages" toggle.
steps.expected: The "Choose the languages you’d like to include." section should be displayed, listing Français, Deutsch, Italiano, and Español.

steps.content: Disable the "Generate policy in multiple languages" toggle.
steps.expected: The "Choose the languages you’d like to include." section should no longer be displayed.

Rewritten to match Free/Agency's majority behavior (toggle enabled by default, disabling hides the section) rather than TrialWithCard's disabled-by-default flavor used in an earlier draft of this case — see the section-wide page-name/toggle-default reconciliation note on the Website details render case.

cases.title: [Cookie Policy Generator] Verify that selecting additional languages under "Choose the languages you’d like to include." excludes the default language and updates the generated policy
cases.preconditions: User is on the Cookie Policy Generator wizard, on the Language settings step, with "English" selected as the default language, the "Generate policy in multiple languages" toggle enabled, and the "Choose the languages you’d like to include." section visible, listing Français, Deutsch, Italiano, and Español.

steps.content: Select one or more languages under "Choose the languages you’d like to include." (e.g. Français).
steps.expected: The selected languages should be checked/highlighted in the list.

steps.content: Verify whether the current default language (English) appears in the additional-languages list.
steps.expected: The default language should not be displayed in the additional-languages list.

steps.content: Change the default language to one of the currently selected additional languages (e.g. Français).
steps.expected: That language should disappear from the additional-languages list, since it is now the default language.

steps.content: Generate the cookie policy after completing the remaining wizard steps.
steps.expected: The cookie policy should be generated for the default language plus each selected additional language.

cases.rewrite_notes: Stripped role prefixes; collapsed 3 plan-clone + 6 role-variant duplicates. Dropped position-only wording (LHS/RHS/bottom-left corner, etc.) per the alignment-drop rule; kept the page title, section list, action buttons, and progress bar as structural elements. cases.title: [Cookie Policy Generator] Verify that the About cookies section's default Heading and Description text can be edited
cases.preconditions: User is on the About cookies step of the Cookie Policy Generator wizard.

steps.content: Observe the default text in the "Heading" field.
steps.expected: The Heading field should default to: "What are cookies?"

steps.content: Observe the default text in the "Description" field.
steps.expected:
The Description field should default to the following text:

"This Cookie Policy explains what cookies are, how we use them, the types of cookies we use (i.e., the information we collect using cookies and how that information is used), and how to manage your cookie settings.

Cookies are small text files used to store small pieces of information. They are stored on your device when a website loads in your browser. These cookies help ensure that the website functions properly, enhance security, provide a better user experience, and analyse performance to identify what works and where improvements are needed."

steps.content: Edit the text in the "Heading" and "Description" fields.
steps.expected: The user should be able to edit both fields freely based on their requirements.

Reproduced the default Description text verbatim per the quoted-text fidelity rule (this is exactly the kind of disclaimer copy the rule exists for) — sourced from TrialWithCard's dossier text; Free/Agency's section_variants showed this field as a blank placeholder in the dossier itself, which reads as incomplete case-authoring rather than a real 'empty by default' behavior, so no plan-based content difference is implied here.

cases.title: [Cookie Policy Generator] Verify that clicking "Next" on the About cookies step with an empty Heading field still navigates to the next step
cases.preconditions: User is on the About cookies step of the Cookie Policy Generator wizard, with the Heading field cleared (empty), showing the "This field cannot be empty." validation error under it.

steps.content: Click the "Next" button at the bottom of the page.
steps.expected: MISMATCH found live: the app does NOT block navigation or scroll to the empty field. Despite the visible validation error, clicking "Next" navigates directly to the "Use of cookies" step, leaving the Heading field empty. Reproduced twice. This may be an actual product bug (client-side required-field validation is cosmetic only and does not gate the wizard's Next action) rather than a migration/wording issue — flagging for human review rather than silently asserting either the old or new behavior as correct.

cases.rewrite_notes: none beyond stripping role prefixes and collapsing plan/role duplicates — identical across all three plan sections, no drift. Companion case ('Verify that clearing the Heading and Description fields...shows a validation error') covers the red-border/error-message assertion; this case covers only the Next-button's scroll-to-field behavior, per the distinct dossier scenario.
cases.audit_note: Quoted strings ("Next" button label, "This field cannot be empty." error text) match Suite 6 source (C13378 et al.) and the confirmed wording in case 39508/39515/39521. The case's real substance is a behavioral MISMATCH note (Next button not gating navigation despite the visible error) which is a live-app/functional concern already flagged via grill_status, not a text-fidelity issue -- out of scope for this audit.
